verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 encodes a large tetrameric Ca²⁺-release channel resident in the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) membrane. Its transmembrane domains face the ER lumen and cytoplasm; there is no extracellular-facing topology at the plasma membrane. Pre-`no` checklist: (1) No cell-surface-directed therapeutic (antibody/ADC/CAR-T) program targeting ITPR3 on the outer leaflet exists. (2) Not a shedding-substrate TM precursor — the protein has no ectodomain exposed extracellularly. (3) No wash-resistant covalent/stable tethering to a PM TM partner documented. (4) No documented PM minority pool — non-ER localizations in cancer are nuclear or MAM (both intracellular). (5) No gene-specific evidence for cell-state-induced outer-leaflet display; no tissue-restricted surface display; not a lysosomal TM protein undergoing exocytosis; no documented vesicular cycling to PM; no activation-induced surface translocation. The HGNC gene-group (IP3 receptors) is a canonically intracellular ER-channel family with no surface biology. All five contextual buckets are ruled out by absence of protein-specific evidence.
